title: arenaria tetraquetra var. microphylla
intro: Arenaria tetraquetra var. microphylla, also known as Dwarf Mountain Sandwort, is a low-growing, succulent perennial plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family and is commonly found in mountainous regions. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Arenaria tetraquetra var. microphylla include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and roots are used to make infusions and poultices for topical application.
